perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, "Confidential Information” means any information, including without limitation, business information, technical, and marketing informati on, pricing, rates and discounts, provided such information is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or is disclosed in a manner that it may be reasonably inferred to be confidential and/or proprietary to the Disclosing Party. In the event of an inadvertent disclosure of source code, such information shall be immediately returned to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, "Confidential Information” means any information, including without limitation, business information, technical, and marketing information, pricing, rates and discounts, regardless of whether such information is explicitly identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. In the event of an inadvertent disclosure of source code, such information shall be handled per Receiving Party's internal policies.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' requires the information to be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure to be considered confidential, while the changed definition broadens it to include any business, technical, or marketing information regardless of explicit identification. This contradicts the original text by removing the requirement for explicit identification. Additionally, instead of requiring immediate return of source code, it leaves it up to the Receiving Party's internal policies, which could potentially conflict with Section 7 'Return of Confidential Information' of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. Upon the completion or termination of any discussions between the parties, or at any time within fourteen (14) days of receipt of a written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall (i) promptly return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information disclosed in tangible form and copies thereof; or (ii) promptly destroy such Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) and certify their destruction to the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing the requirement for explicit identification at the time of disclosure. This contradicts the original text and the contradicted section 'Return of Confidential Information'. Additionally, the altered handling of inadvertently disclosed source code, now subject to the Receiving Party's internal policies instead of immediate return, introduces a direct conflict with the 'Return of Confidential Information' clause, which mandates a specific return or destruction process. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be retained in confidence by the Receiving Party and used only for the Purpose. Confidential Information may be disclosed only to employees or consultants of the Receiving Party who have a need to know. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Receiving Party is a firm that provides outsourced or "third party" logistics services to companies for part, or sometimes all , of their function, Receiving Party may disclose its rates to clients expressly authorized and eligible to receive such information through a bona fide authentication or registration process maintained by Receiving P arty.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be retained in confidence by the Receiving Party and used only for the Purpose. Confidential Information may be disclosed to any employee or consultant of the Receiving Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Receiving Party is a firm that provides outsourced or "third party" logistics services to companies for part, or sometimes all, of their function, Receiving Party may disclose its rates to all of its clients.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition restricted the disclosure of Confidential Information to employees/consultants with a 'need to know'. The modified version broadens this to 'any employee or consultant'. Additionally, the original contract allowed disclosure of rates only to clients 'expressly authorized and eligible' through a 'bona fide authentication or registration process.' The modified version allows disclosure to 'all of its clients,' regardless of authorization or authentication. This creates a contradiction by widening the scope of who can access Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Use Restrictions. Any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be retained in confidence by the Receiving Party and used only for the Purpose. Confidential Information may be disclosed to any employee or consultant of the Receiving Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Receiving Party is a firm that provides outsourced or "third party" logistics services to companies for part, or sometimes all, of their function, Receiving Party may disclose its rates to all of its clients. Any consultant or client of Receiving Party who receives Confidential Information under this Agreement shall be similarly bound in writing to a nondisclosure agreement no less restrictive than the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by an employee or consultant of such Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but no less than reasonable care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted disclosure to employees/consultants with a 'need to know' and to clients 'expressly authorized and eligible' through a specific process. The changed text broadens this to 'any employee or consultant' and 'all of its clients' without authorization. The contradicted text includes a clause that 'Any consultant or client of Receiving Party who receives Confidential Information under this Agreement shall be similarly bound in writing to a nondisclosure agreement no less restrictive than the terms of this Agreement.' By disclosing to all clients without the need for authorization, the changed text contradicts the requirement that only those bound by a nondisclosure agreement should receive confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated by either party at any time upon thi rty (30) days written notice. All obligations hereunder shall continue for five (5) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate two (2) years from the Effective Date and cannot be terminated earlier. All obligations hereunder shall continue indefinitely from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement terminates in two years but allows termination by either party with 30 days' notice. The modified version eliminates the early termination clause. It also changes the duration of obligations from five years to 'indefinitely'. This creates a direct contradiction in terms of termination options and the duration of obligations under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Duration. This Agreement will terminate two (2) years from the Effective Date and cannot be terminated earlier. All obligations hereunder shall continue indefinitely from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination clause, eliminating the option for early termination with 30 days' notice and extending the duration of obligations from five years to 'indefinitely'. This directly contradicts the original clause, which allowed for early termination and a fixed duration of obligations. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the change creates a self-contradiction by removing the early termination and setting obligations to 'indefinitely' when it should have been two years.

perturbation.original_text: If the Receiving Party becomes subject to a demand for discovery or disclosure of the Confidential Information of t he other party under legal process, such Receiving Party shall give to the other prompt notice of the demand prior to furnishing the Confidential Information demanded, and, at the expense of the Disclosing Party, shall obtain or cooperate with the Disclosi ng Party in seeking reasonable arrangements to protect the confidential and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Receiving Party becomes subject to a demand for discovery or disclosure of the Confidential Information of the other party under legal process, the Receiving Party shall immediately furnish the Confidential Information demanded without notifying the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party before furnishing Confidential Information under legal process and to cooperate in protecting it. The modified version instructs the Receiving Party to immediately disclose the information without notification. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the Receiving Party's obligations when facing a legal demand for Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Legal Process. If the Receiving Party becomes subject to a demand for discovery or disclosure of the Confidential Information of the other party under legal process, the Receiving Party shall immediately furnish the Confidential Information demanded without notifying the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text required notification and cooperation before disclosure, whereas the changed text mandates immediate disclosure without notification. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, creating a strong contradiction with the original intent of notification and cooperation. Therefore, the modification introduces a clear and direct conflict within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall remain the exclusiv e property of the Disclosing Party and nothing contained herein shall be construed as a grant, express or implied or by estoppel, of a transfer, assignment, license, lease of any right, title or interest in the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall become the shared property of both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, granting the Receiving Party a non-exclusive license to use the information for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The modified version states that the Confidential Information becomes shared property and grants a non-exclusive license to the Receiving Party. This is a direct contradiction as it changes the ownership and usage rights of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall become the shared property of both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, granting the Receiving Party a non-exclusive license to use the information for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Confidential Information becomes 'shared property' and grants a 'non-exclusive license' to the Receiving Party, directly contradicting the contradicted text which mirrors that statement. This is a clear conflict regarding the ownership and usage rights of Confidential Information, as it no longer remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party as initially stated.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is valid only when signed by an employee with authority to bind that party. For YRC , this Agreement when unmodified may be signed by any Manager or higher in rank. However, if this Agreement is modified, it shall only be effective when accompanied by the YRC Legal Department’s signature or stamp of approval.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is valid when signed by any employee of either party, regardless of their authority or position. The signature of the YRC Legal Department is advisory but not required for any modifications.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the agreement is valid only when signed by an employee with binding authority and that modifications require YRC Legal's signature. The modified version states the agreement is valid with any employee's signature and that YRC Legal's signature is merely advisory. This creates a direct contradiction regarding who has the authority to bind the parties and what is required for modifications to be valid, creating ambiguity in contract enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Authorized Signature. This Agreement is valid when signed by any employee of either party, regardless of their authority or position. The signature of the YRC Legal Department is advisory but not required for any modifications. YRC Freight By: Name: Title: Address: 10990 Roe Avenue Overland Park, Kansas 66211 USA Tel: (913) ____- _____ Fax: (913) ____- _______ Fax NDA to: (913) 982-5436 E-Business Your Company Name: Website that will host API: Requested API: My Y RC Username: Name: Signature: E Mail Address: Title: Address: Tel: Fax: For Int ernal Use Only: BusId #: _______________________________________ Approval Date: _____________________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original clause and the contradicted text by stating that any employee can sign the agreement, regardless of authority, and that YRC Legal's signature is advisory, while the original text and contradicted text imply or require authorized signatures and legal approval for modifications. This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict regarding who can bind the parties and the validity of modifications.
